summary: Wave 2/5: commerce & data — ownership+real-price+buy-links+CSV, catalog browse UX (grid/list, favorites, wishlist), and sharing (compressed share-link, named saves, templates), with expert implementer→review→fix loops
logs: Wave 2: final integration + full test pass...

findings.issue: Clicking the 'own' star on a catalog row jumps the catalog scroll back to the top. toggleOwned() in app.js calls catalogUI.refresh() (=draw()), which does els.list.innerHTML='' on the .catalog element — which is itself the overflow:auto/max-height:530px scroll container (id catalog-list) — so scrollTop resets to 0. The inline star handler already updates the button locally, and catalog.js's comment wrongly claims 'no redraw, so scroll position is kept'; the full refresh is only actually needed to reflect summary-driven toggles back onto catalog stars.
findings.fix: Have refresh preserve/restore scroll (save els.list.scrollTop before innerHTML='' and restore after), or skip the catalog refresh when the toggle originated from a catalog row (the inline handler already restyles it), only refreshing when toggled from the summary list. Also correct the misleading comment.

findings.issue: In the summary 'Sets & ownership' list, the per-set price button shows the LINE total (money(ln.lineTotal) = qty × unit), but clicking it prompts for a per-SET (unit) price ('Your price for set N (USD)'). For qty>1 this is confusing: a row showing $119.98 for qty 2 will, if the user re-enters 119.98, be treated as the unit price (→ $239.96 line).
findings.fix: Show the unit price on the button (or label it clearly as line total and prompt with the current unit price prefilled — it already prefills overrides[n], but the visible number differs from what's being edited). Minor; single-qty sets are unaffected.

review.testSummary: npm test: 56 tests pass, 0 fail (was 46 before bundle; +10 new tests in test/pricing.test.mjs covering baseNum, resolvePrice override>msrp>estimate, cityCost owned/buy split + estimate flags + override + empty city, buyLinks for sets vs generic pieces, bricklinkXml, setListCsv RFC-4180 quoting). All assert concrete values — none vacuous. node --check --input-type=module passes for pricing.js, app.js, catalog.js, data.js, summary.js.
review.specCoverage: All spec items implemented. PLAN-1: 'bcp.owned' Set of bare set numbers; star toggle on catalog rows AND summary per-set list; summary budget split into 'Already own $0 (struck MSRP)' vs 'Still to buy'; owned excluded from all exports. PLAN-2: data/prices.json seeded with ~47 real City/Modular/Train MSRPs (keys are bare nums, _note string key harmlessly ignored by lookup); loaded in data.js and attached as s.price; pure cityCost(placed,{prices,owned,overrides,pieces}) in js/pricing.js returns {ownedCost,buyCost,estimatedCount,estimatedBuyCount,total,lines}; real→override→estimate precedence; 'bcp.prices' override via prompt; summary shows count of estimated to-buy sets; catalog price-asc/price-desc sort added. PLAN-5: LEGO/BrickLink/Amazon buy links on catalog rows + in txt export; generic 'piece-' items map to BrickLink part search; all target=_blank rel=noopener, keyboard-reachable <a>. PLAN-9: BrickLink Wanted-List XML (<INVENTORY><ITEM>… ITEMTYPE S, ITEMID <num>-1, MINQTY qty) and CSV (number,name,qty,price) buttons in a 3-tile export grid; generic pieces skipped in XML; owned excluded. Plain-text set list still works. All client-side, zero runtime deps, vanilla ESM. Accessibility: real <button>s with aria-pressed/aria-label, labeled <select>. No regressions to undo/redo, selection/groups, drag/rotate/resize, layers, snapping, catalog drag-drop, save/restore, autosave pill, or shortcuts modal (owned/price state kept outside placed[]/undo).

review.testSummary: `npm test` (node --test): 62/62 pass, 0 fail (46 pre-existing + 16 new: 6 in test/lists.test.mjs for pushRecent, 10 in test/pricing.test.mjs for baseNum/resolvePrice/cityCost/buyLinks/bricklinkXml/setListCsv). All 6 changed/new JS modules pass `node --check --input-type=module` (app.js, catalog.js, data.js, summary.js, pricing.js, lists.js). Also functionally verified live: served the app locally, seeded bcp.wishlist/bcp.favorites/bcp.recent/bcp.catView via localStorage, and screenshotted the real render — grid view, the recent/favorites rail (with real thumbnails), and the wishlist panel (promote ➕ / remove ✕, correct subtotal, independent of the empty grid's budget block) all render and behave correctly.
review.specCoverage:
All three spec items are genuinely implemented and working, not stubs:
- UI-4 (grid/list toggle): #catalog-view segmented control, pure-CSS `.catalog.view-grid` layout reusing the same `.set` rows, choice persisted to localStorage 'bcp.catView', survives search/sort re-renders because the class lives on the container (not reset by draw()). Verified live.
- QOL-7 (recent + favorites rail): `.rail` strip above the catalog list, tracks last 8 distinct placed sets via a new pure `pushRecent()` (js/lists.js, unit-tested) keyed 'bcp.recent', separate ♡/♥ favorite toggle on every row keyed 'bcp.favorites'; both strips place via the same `placeSet()`→`grid.addSet/addSetAt` path used by drag-drop and the ＋ button. Verified live with real thumbnails.
- PLAN-6 (wishlist): collapsible `<details class="wishlist-panel">` in the summary card, keyed 'bcp.wishlist' (full set_num), its own subtotal via `cityCost()` (reused, not a parallel implementation), fully independent of the grid budget block (renders even when the grid is empty), 'Place on grid' promote button (deliberately doesn't remove the entry) and a remove ✕ button. Verified live.
- The three star-ish affordances (☆/★ owned, ♡/♥ favorite, 🛒 wishlist) are visually and semantically distinct, each documented in a code comment explaining the split — satisfies the "coordinate the star affordances" instruction well.
- All new localStorage keys are namespaced under 'bcp.'. All new user/catalog-data string interpolation into innerHTML goes through esc(); the one unescaped `s.img` src is a pre-existing pattern (unchanged from before this bundle) tied to build-time catalog data, not user input.
- No regressions found in undo/redo, selection/groups, align/distribute, drag/rotate/resize, layers, snapping, catalog drag-drop, save/restore/autosave, or the shortcuts modal — grid.js/storage.js were untouched by this diff and their test suites still pass; app.js's new placeSet()/toggle* functions correctly route through the existing addSet/addSetAt commit path so undo/history is unaffected (owned/favorites/wishlist/recent are correctly kept outside placed[]/undo, per the ship rules).

findings.issue: In the wishlist panel's <summary> header (js/summary.js ~line 105, styled by css/styles.css line 404 `.wishlist-panel summary .wish-total{margin-left:auto;...}`), both `.count-pill` (line 109, also `margin-left:auto`) and `.wish-total` sit in the same flex row with independent `margin-left:auto`. Per the CSS flexbox spec, free space is split evenly across *all* auto margins on the line, so the leftover width is divided in half: one half pushes the count pill away from the 'Wishlist' label, the other half pushes the price away from the count pill. Verified visually with a real headless render (localStorage-seeded wishlist, 2 items, $419.98 subtotal): the '2' badge and the '$419.98' total end up with a large, clearly unintended gap between them instead of sitting together with the price flush to the right edge.
findings.fix: Remove the auto margin from the count pill in this specific context so only the last element (wish-total) absorbs the free space, e.g. add `.wishlist-panel summary .count-pill{margin-left:0}` after the shared `.count-pill` rule (or after line 404), leaving `.wish-total`'s `margin-left:auto` to push the price to the far right while the pill stays snug next to the 'Wishlist' label.

review.testSummary: npm test: 76/76 pass, 0 fail (node --test). Also ran `node --check --input-type=module` on every touched/added module (js/app.js, js/storage.js, js/catalog.js, js/data.js, js/summary.js, js/share.js, js/pricing.js, js/lists.js) — all clean. test/share.test.mjs (new) covers encodeCity/decodeCity round-trip on both the gzip path (this Node 24 env has CompressionStream, so it genuinely exercises gzip, not just the fallback) and the forced-fallback path, tag-prefix distinctness, URL-fragment-safety, and 6 malformed/garbage-input rejection cases. test/storage.test.mjs gained real coverage for the new renameCity/loadCities/deleteCity exports, including a case distinguishing 'current pointer follows when it pointed at the renamed city' vs 'left alone otherwise'.
review.specCoverage: QOL-5a (share link): fully correct. encodeCity/decodeCity in js/share.js are pure, gzip-when-available with a deterministic uncompressed base64url fallback tagged distinctly ('g'/'0'), fully async on both paths as required, and decodeCity returns null on any malformed/garbage/wrong-tag/bad-JSON input (verified by tests + code read). 'Copy share link' writes `origin+pathname+'#d='+payload`, uses clipboard.writeText with a prompt() fallback, toasts on success. QOL-5b (read-only-first shared link): fully correct. checkSharedHash() runs at the very end of boot(), strictly after the normal autosave-restore path, decodes+shape-validates via storage.js's existing validateCity(), always consumes the hash via history.replaceState, and only shows a banner (textContent-only, no innerHTML) with an explicit 'Import into my planner' confirm button — the live grid is never touched until that click, and importSharedCity() correctly flushAutosave()s the current work first. QOL-5c (named saves): mostly correct — Save as / My cities menu / switch / delete all correctly flush the live grid before switching and are wired to the real storage.js multi-city API. However the rename path has a genuine, empirically-verified data-loss bug (see findings) when renaming a different saved city onto the name of the currently-open one. QOL-6 (starter templates): fully correct. data/templates.json ships 5 curated cities (Town Square, Train Loop, Modular Street, Fire & Police Block, Airport Plaza — within the 4-6 range) built entirely from real catalog set numbers; I verified programmatically that every set_num resolves in the live catalog, every template's placed[] has zero geometry overlaps, and every template's bbox exactly matches its declared grid size (no out-of-bounds tiles). The 'New from Template' modal sits right next to '＋ New city' in the topbar as specified, has a real overwrite-guard confirm only when the current grid is non-empty, and reuses the existing dismissFirstRun()/autosave()/toast() plumbing the same way the pre-existing 'Load a sample city' flow does.

findings.issue: Data-loss bug in the 'My cities' rename flow (js/app.js renameSavedCity(), ~lines 527-539, backed by storage.js renameCity()). Renaming a DIFFERENT saved city onto the name of the currently-open city silently overwrites that name's on-disk slot with the other city's data, while the live in-memory grid for the open city is left completely untouched. renameCity()'s 'follow the current pointer' logic is keyed off the OLD name (`currentCityName() === oldName`), and app.js's own post-rename guard is `if (cityName === oldName)` — both are false in this cross-city-collision case, so nothing flushes the live grid or reloads it from the new disk state. I verified this empirically against the real js/storage.js: after the rename, loadCity(theOpenCityName) returns the OTHER city's placed[]/grid, not the open city's. The generic confirm dialog ('"X" already exists. Overwrite it?') never warns the user that X is their currently-open city.
findings.failure_scenario: City 'Active City' is open and its saved slot matches disk. A separate saved city 'Side City' also exists. User opens My cities, clicks rename (✎) on 'Side City', types 'Active City' as the new name, and confirms the overwrite prompt. Storage now holds Side City's tiles under the name 'Active City'; the on-screen grid still shows the real Active City tiles but is now out of sync with disk. If the user closes the tab (or the browser) before making any further edit — the only thing that would trigger an autosave and incidentally repair the slot — the original Active City layout is unrecoverable on next load.
findings.fix: After a successful renameCity() call, check whether the NEW name equals the currently-open cityName (regardless of which side of the rename that was) and, if so, reconcile the live view with what's now actually on disk — e.g. reload the grid from loadCity(next) the same way loadSavedCity() does (or, more conservatively, refuse the rename with a specific error like "Can't rename onto your currently-open city; save or switch away from it first" instead of the generic overwrite confirm).

findings.issue: buildTemplatesMenu() in js/app.js unconditionally shows 'Templates failed to load.' whenever `templates.length === 0`, which conflates a genuinely-empty (but successfully fetched) templates.json with an actual fetch/parse failure — both paths fall back to the same `templates = []`.
findings.failure_scenario: If data/templates.json is ever shipped as `[]` (or all templates are later removed), the gallery falsely tells the user the templates failed to load instead of showing an accurate empty-state message, even though nothing actually errored.
findings.fix: Track load success separately from emptiness, e.g. a `templatesLoadFailed` boolean set only in the catch block / on a non-ok response, and branch the empty-list message in buildTemplatesMenu() on that flag instead of on `templates.length === 0`.

result.integrationReport:
Everything integrates cleanly — no fixes were required. Full verification results below.

## Verification

**`node --check --input-type=module` — all 13 modules PASS:**
`app.js, catalog.js, data.js, geometry.js, grid.js, lists.js, pricing.js, schematic.js, share.js, storage.js, summary.js, units.js, util.js` — every one OK, zero syntax errors.

**`npm test` — 76/76 PASS** (0 fail, 0 skipped; ~580ms). The wave-1 baseline of 46 grew to 76; the three bundles added `pricing.test.mjs`, `share.test.mjs`, `lists.test.mjs`, and expanded `storage.test.mjs` (rename/delete/loadCities).

**Structural checks:** no duplicate element IDs in index.html; legal footer intact; all app.js-referenced IDs exist (catalog-view/rail/sort, shared-banner + import/dismiss, btn-share, templates-*, cities-*, city-name-text, btn-save-as); CSS has hooks for every new feature (view-grid, rail-item, wishlist-panel, shared-banner, export-grid, tpl-card, city-row). No `?v=` cache-busting — correct, this project doesn't use that convention.

## Features shipped this wave + user-facing controls

**commerce-core**
- Owned toggle — ☆/★ button on each catalog row and in the summary's "Sets & ownership" list; owned sets are excluded from buy cost and all exports.
- Prices + manual override — real MSRP shown when known, else `≈$` estimate (~$0.11/piece); click a set's price in the summary to set/clear a personal USD override (prompt).
- Budget split — summary shows "Still to buy" vs "Already own ($0)" with an estimated-count note.
- Buy links — LEGO / BrickLink / Amazon links per catalog row and in the .txt set list.
- Exports (summary card, owned excluded) — 🧾 Set list .txt, 📄 CSV, 🧱 BrickLink Wanted-List .xml.

**catalog-browse-ux**
- List/grid view toggle — segmented control above the catalog (`data-view` list/grid), remembered in `bcp.catView`.
- Recently-used + favorites rail — strip pinned above the catalog list; ♡/♥ favorite button per row; recent auto-tracks the last 8 distinct placed sets; both re-place with one click.
- Wishlist — 🛒 button per row; a collapsible "🛒 Wishlist" panel in the summary with its own subtotal, plus ➕ place-on-grid and ✕ remove.
- Sort dropdown — default / name / price ↑↓ / pieces / year.

**sharing-persistence**
- Compressed share link — 🔗 Share button; builds a `#d=` URL (gzip when available, plain base64url fallback), copies to clipboard.
- Shared-link banner — opening a `#d=` URL shows a confirm-before-overwrite banner (Import / Dismiss); never touches the live grid until the user opts in.
- Named saves — "💾 Save as", a "My cities" menu (📂 load / ✎ rename / 🗑 delete) reachable from btn-cities and the topbar city-name label.
- Templates gallery — "Templates" button opens a modal of curated starter cities (`data/templates.json`) with auto-generated SVG thumbnails.

## New data files
- `data/prices.json` — 55 seeded USD MSRPs (bare-number keys).
- `data/templates.json` — curated starter cities (~31 KB).

## Caveats before browser-testing
- **Real prices:** only **46** of the 55 price entries match a set actually in the catalog (**47 catalog rows** get a real MSRP — City/Modular/Train seeds like 60316, 10270, 10312, 71044-family, etc.). Everything else falls back to the `≈$0.11/piece` estimate flagged "est." **9 price keys are dead** (no matching catalog set, silently unused): `10194, 10219, 10233, 10254, 10308, 21058, 31120, 31139, 71044`. Harmless, but they buy nothing — worth pruning or adding the sets later.
- **Share link without CompressionStream:** encode/decode round-trips on any single browser (gzip when present, `0`-tagged base64url fallback otherwise). The gap is cross-browser: a `g`-tagged (gzipped) link created on a Streams-capable browser and opened on one lacking `DecompressionStream` decodes to null → "share link looks invalid" toast, rather than transparently. Same-capability and all-fallback cases are fine.
- **No state collisions:** the three localStorage-star concerns are deliberately distinct keys/glyphs — `bcp.owned` (☆/★), `bcp.favorites` (♡/♥), `bcp.wishlist` (🛒), plus `bcp.recent`, `bcp.catView`, `bcp.prices`, `bcp.cities`/`bcp.current`, `bcp.firstRunSeen`. No duplicates. All owned/favorite/wishlist/price state lives outside `placed[]`/undo, as required.
- **No share-vs-restore race:** `checkSharedHash()` is awaited last in `boot()`, after the autosave-restore, and only raises a banner — the restored city loads normally and the shared city waits for explicit user import.
